talk_metadata.talk_title: Genetics and environmental risk factors of lung adenocarcinoma in East Asia
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "never smokers shows better prognosis when we compare with smokers"
Speaker said: "the EGFR mutation is higher in smokers, but even in the smokers, there are so many EGFR positive patient"
Speaker said: "we are doing whole genome sequencing analysis of lung adenocarcinoma of smokers and never smokers"

key_data_gaps: No clinical outcome data linking identified germline risk factors to therapy response

unresolved_questions: How do identified germline polymorphisms mechanistically drive EGFR‑positive tumor initiation?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Germline susceptibility to lung cancer in never smokers
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "the heritability of lung cancer has been estimated at 18% with substantial contribution from shared environment"
Speaker said: "we identified TP53 as the strongest rare variant signal in young‑onset lung cancer"
Speaker said: "BRCA2 germline pathogenic variant with loss of heterozygosity led to exceptional response to platinum and PARP inhibition"

vulnerability_identified.failing_compound_or_target: PARP inhibitor monotherapy in BRCA2‑deficient lung cancer
vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Limited data on durability of PARP response in lung tissue microenvironment
vulnerability_identified.ghost_responder_admission: exceptionally well to platinum‑based chemotherapy followed by PARP inhibitor

trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Enrolling based on family history without systematic population screening may miss carriers
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: Comprehensive germline EGFR T790M testing in at‑risk relatives
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Limited power to assess cancer incidence due to modest cohort size

key_data_gaps: Lack of longitudinal exposure data linking air pollution to germline risk
No randomized comparison of screening strategies in EGFR T790M carriers

crispro_opportunity.description: Identify polygenic risk score thresholds that predict EGFR‑positive lung cancer in never smokers beyond known germline variants.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "polygenic risk scores for lung adenocarcinoma were associated with a small amount of risk"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate GWAS‑derived PRS with somatic mutation data to refine high‑risk subpopulations.
external_validation_needed: PubMed: recent GWAS of lung cancer in never smokers

crispro_opportunity.description: Target TP53‑mutated, EGFR‑positive tumors where TP53 loss may drive genomic instability.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "TP53 emerged as the strongest signal among all lung cancers"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model TP53‑driven mutational processes to predict response to DNA‑damage agents.
external_validation_needed: Preclinical studies of TP53‑restoring compounds in EGFR‑mutant lung models

cited_competitors.name: Invitae
cited_competitors.context: provider of clinical germline multigene panel testing

unresolved_questions: What is the quantitative contribution of second‑hand smoke versus air pollution to germline‑driven risk?
Can structural variant burden improve risk prediction beyond SNV‑based PRS?

assets_to_track: BRCA2 germline pathogenic variants in lung cancer

talk_metadata.talk_title: Differential response to anticancer treatment depending on smoking status in lung cancer
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "immune checkpoint inhibitor monotherapy has a very less benefit to the lung cancer in never smoker"
Speaker said: "EGFR TKI benefit is larger in never smoker and ALK is very similar"
Speaker said: "DDR1 expression separates PFS from immune checkpoint monotherapy in never smoker adenocarcinoma"

vulnerability_identified.failing_compound_or_target: Immune checkpoint inhibitor monotherapy in never‑smoker NSCLC
vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Low tumor mutation burden and PD‑L1 expression not fully explaining lack of response
vulnerability_identified.unexplained_resistance_quote: Speaker said: "some lung cancer in never smoker patient have risk of unnecessary, sometimes harmful due to hyperprogression immune checkpoint inhibitor therapy"
vulnerability_identified.ghost_responder_admission: patient with PBAP loss‑of‑function mutation responded dramatically despite low PD‑L1

trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: Efficacy of ICI ± chemotherapy in never‑smoker vs smoker NSCLC
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Mixing heterogeneous histologies and driver‑mutation statuses without stratification dilutes signal
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: Comprehensive TMB and PD‑L1 combined with DDR1 or MMR‑transcriptomic signatures
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Underpowered subgroup analyses for rare biomarkers (e.g., PBAP, DDR1 high)

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Speaker noted benefit is more prominent in smokers, indicating limited added value for never‑smoker population
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Pursuing broad anti‑angiogenic addition may dilute efficacy signal in EGFR‑mutant never smokers

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed low PD‑L1 and TMB in never smokers yet concluded that immune checkpoint inhibitors should still be offered, despite data showing minimal benefit

key_data_gaps: Lack of prospective randomized trials separating never‑smoker subcohorts
Limited mechanistic validation of DDR1 and PBAP as predictive biomarkers

crispro_opportunity.description: Develop DDR1‑targeted agents or biomarkers to select never‑smoker patients for ICI therapy.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "DDR1 is a very important marker for T cell infiltration"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model DDR1‑mediated immune exclusion pathways to prioritize combination strategies.
external_validation_needed: Preclinical studies of DDR1 inhibition in EGFR‑mutant NSCLC models

crispro_opportunity.description: Use PBAP loss‑of‑function mutation as a predictive biomarker for ICI response in never smokers.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "PBAP mutation, especially loss of function mutation, is around 10% of the lung cancer population and never smoker population"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate PBAP mutation status with transcriptomic signatures to generate a response score.
external_validation_needed: Retrospective validation in independent ICI cohorts

cited_competitors.name: Amivantamab
cited_competitors.context: mentioned as standard of care for EGFR exon 20 insertion

unresolved_questions: What is the optimal biomarker panel (PD‑L1, TMB, DDR1, MMR‑transcriptomic) to predict ICI benefit in never smokers?
How does whole‑genome doubling mechanistically drive ICI resistance?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Molecular and evolutionary landscape of never‑smoker lung adenocarcinoma (Sherlock study)
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we identified three subtypes that we call piano, mezzo‑forte, and forte with increasing genomic alterations"
Speaker said: "ID2 deletion in a homopolymer of T was strongly correlated with tumor proliferation markers"
Speaker said: "EGFR mutant tumors had many more structural variants than KRAS mutant tumors"

vulnerability_identified.failing_compound_or_target: Standard EGFR TKIs in EGFR‑mutant tumors with high copy‑number alteration burden
vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Complex structural variants may confer resistance not captured by mutation testing alone

trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: Prognostic classification of never‑smoker lung adenocarcinoma
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Combining smokers and never smokers in the same analysis may mask subtype‑specific signals
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: Methylation signatures distinguishing AT1‑origin vs AT2‑origin tumors
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Multiple testing across 146 gene sets increases false‑positive risk

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Requires whole‑transcriptome profiling, limiting clinical scalability
competitive_moat_weakness.scale_or_manufacturing_bottleneck: High‑throughput RNA‑seq cost and turnaround time
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Reliance on complex bioinformatic pipelines hampers adoption in community settings

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker presented that EGFR mutations are indolent yet also reported that EGFR‑mutant tumors have many copy‑number alterations, suggesting aggressive genomic instability

key_data_gaps: Prospective validation of the 60‑gene prognostic panel

crispro_opportunity.description: Leverage CrisPRO to integrate copy‑number, structural variant, and methylation data to refine the piano/mezzo‑forte/forte classification without full RNA‑seq.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "EGFR mutant tumors had many more structural variants than KRAS mutant tumors"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO’s multi‑omics fusion engine can generate a reduced‑feature model predictive of outcome.
external_validation_needed: Independent cohort with matched WGS and clinical outcomes

crispro_opportunity.description: Target the epigenetic constraints in AT1‑origin EGFR tumors (e.g., DNA methylation modulators).
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "we tested methylation probes that define AT1 vs AT2 cell of origin"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can predict which EGFR tumors retain AT1 epigenetic signatures and prioritize epigenetic therapy.
external_validation_needed: Preclinical studies of DNMT inhibitors in AT1‑origin EGFR models

cited_competitors.name: Stanford group (Jewell, Desai)
cited_competitors.context: proposed AT1 cell of origin hypothesis for EGFR‑mutant tumors

unresolved_questions: What proportion of EGFR‑mutant never‑smoker tumors truly arise from AT1 cells versus AT2 cells?
Can the ID2 deletion signature be used prospectively to identify high‑risk aggressive tumors?
